mechanism_narrative: Higher nominal interest rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and raise debt-service costs, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over two months signals weakening aggregate demand and impending earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are nonnegative in greater than 55 percent of triggered observations over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth slows while the federal funds rate remains elevated, the interaction of contracting demand and restrictive monetary policy amplifies downside risk to corporate revenues, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) during joint high-FEDFUNDS and low-RSAFS episodes, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: During an active monetary tightening cycle, a month-over-month increase in consumer prices accelerates expectations of further hawkish policy, raising equity risk premiums and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in greater than 55 percent of tightening-cycle months where CPIAUCSL rises.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in residential construction starts below 1,450 thousand units indicates weakening housing sector momentum that drags broad economic activity and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than half of observations when HOUST is below 1450 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls decline simultaneously with a rising unemployment rate, the joint deterioration in labor market breadth and household income confidence triggers risk-off repositioning, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS decreases and UNRATE increases month-over-month
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are nonnegative in greater than 50 percent of months where both PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate revenue growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are negative in greater than 55 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level below 5.0 percent signals a less restrictive monetary stance that reduces discount rates and credit costs, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 55 percent of triggered instances where FEDFUNDS drops below 5.0 percent.
